'''Semi Valemei''' (born 20 January 1999) is a Fijian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] at international level.

He has previously played for the [[Canberra Raiders]] and [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Born in [[Labasa]], Fiji, Valemei attended [[Seaqaqa]] Central College and [[Natabua High School]], where he played [[rugby union]]. 

In 2017, he was scouted by former [[National Rugby League|NRL]] player [[Wise Kativerata]] and moved to [[Australia]] to play [[rugby league]]. He moved to the [[Sunshine Coast, Queensland]], playing for the Caloundra Sharks before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://fijisun.com.fj/2020/05/23/semi-ready-for-raiders/|title=Semi ready for Raiders|date=23 May 2020|website=Fiji Times}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pressreader.com/australia/sunshine-coast-daily/20170612/282273845343950|title=Results - Sunshine Coast|date=12 June 2017|website=Sunshine Coast Daily}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In 2018, Valemei played for the Raiders' [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] feeder side, [[Mounties RLFC|Mounties]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2018/07/17/team-lists-mounties2/|title=Team Lists: Mounties|date=17 July 2018|website=Canberra Raiders}}</ref>

In 2019, he played for Mounties in the [[New South Wales Cup]] and for the Raiders in the Jersey Flegg Cup, scoring a hat-trick in their Grand Final loss to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2019/09/29/south-sydney-crowned-2019-jersey-flegg-cup-champions/|title=South Sydney crowned 2019 Jersey Flegg Cup champions|date=29 September 2019|website=NSWRL}}</ref> In October 2019, he represented [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] at the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/world-cup/mens-squads-finalised-ahead-of-2019-nines-world-cup/news-story/de21255466435a929106a5ec22876be0|title=Nines World Cup squads announced|date=17 October 2019|website=Fox Sports}}</ref>

===2020===
In round 10 of the [[2020 NRL season]], Valemei was named to make his NRL debut against the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2020/07/15/semi-valemei-to-make-nrl-debut/|title=Semi Valemei to make NRL debut|date=15 July 2020|website=Canberra Raiders}}</ref>

He played in all three of Canberra's finals matches including the preliminary final defeat to [[Melbourne Storm]]. He played a total of ten games in his debut season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/ricky-stuart-storms-out-of-canberra-raiders-press-conference-after-one-question/edab3d4d-0eeb-4960-acc0-89d06797f8e0/|title=Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart storms out of press conference after one question|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

===2021===
Valemei made eleven appearances for Canberra in the [[2021 NRL season]] which saw the club finish 10th on the table and miss out on the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-canberra-raiders-vs-sydney-roosters-live-blog-live-stream-supercoach-scores-videos-jack-wighton-james-tedesco/news-story/745b280dcc746da257fecd8fda231afd/|title=Roosters' 'Mr Fix It' stuns with hat-trick, Sloppy Raiders' final fade out ends season: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=2 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2021-roosters-v-canberra-adam-keighran-stars-as-raiders-season-ends-with-shocking-defeat/news-story/f9ef95652ed66e97e4e047245f11da7b/|title=Tough conversations coming for senior Canberra stars as interest grows in Parramatta young gun Dylan Brown|website=www.theaustralian.com.au}}</ref>

===2023===
On 12 May, Valemei was granted a release from his Raiders contract and joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] on a one-and-a-half-year contract.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2023/05/12/cowboys-sign-semi-valemei-immediate-effect/|title=Cowboys sign Valemei|date=12 May 2023|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref> Valemei scored a [[hat-trick]] on his club debut for the Cowboys in their 45–20 win over Melbourne during round 14 at [[North Queensland Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-north-queensland-cowboys-vs-melbourne-storm-live-stream-live-blog-supercoach-scores-videos-valentine-holmes-cameron-munster/news-story/8f27ef06bb6f8ba4a85d0d3761c06bef/|title=Bellamy calls out ‘putrid’ Storm after 20-year low as new Cowboys signing explodes — Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.au}}</ref> He played nine matches for North Queensland in the [[2023 NRL season]] and scored nine tries as the club finished 11th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-north-queensland-cowboys-season-review/7022566a2d89154cc990318b#:~:text=They%20won%20just%20four%20of,the%20nadir%20of%20their%20efforts./|title=NRL 2023: North Queensland Cowboys season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

===2024===
He played eighteen games for North Queensland in the [[2024 NRL season]] as they finished 5th on the table. He played in both finals games for North Queensland as they were eliminated in the second week by [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-north-queensland-cowboys-season-review-20240924-p5kcxq.html/|title=The Mole: Cowboys show improvement in 2024 but worrying finals stat sparks thorny question|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref> On 21 August, Valemei re-signed with the Cowboys until the end of 2025.

===2025===
Valemei played just four NRL games in an injury-interrupted [[2025 North Queensland Cowboys season|2025 season]]. He scored four tries, which included a double against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in his final game for North Queensland.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2025/09/03/cowboys-to-farewell-seven-departing-players/|title=Cowboys to farewell seven departing players|date=3 September 2025|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2025/08/30/super-saturday-raiders-v-wests-tigers-dragons-v-sea-eagles-cowboys-v-broncos/|title=Broncos in top four; Raiders crowned minor premiers; Manly stay alive|date=30 August 2025|website=NRL}}</ref>

On 3 September, he signed for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year contract.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article/51/valemei-signs-for-tigers-on-a-two-year-deal|title=Valemei Signs For Tigers On A Two Year Deal|date=3 September 2025|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2 September 2025 |title=Castleford sign Fiji winger Valemei |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce9394njmjpo |access-date=21 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

=== 2026 ===
Valemei was assigned Castleford's number 2 shirt for the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=26 December 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2026 squad numbers with new-look top 17 and shocks confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2026-squad-numbers-with-new-look-top-17-and-shocks-confirmed |access-date=21 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> In January, he suffered a hamstring strain during pre-season training, ruling him out of the opening rounds.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=23 January 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers dealt pre-season injury blow as star import suffers setback |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-dealt-pre-season-injury-blow-as-star-import-suffers-setback |access-date=21 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He was first included in Castleford's 21-man squad in March.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=12 March 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers v St Helens: former NRL ace in debut contention as Challenge Cup 21-man squads named |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-v-st-helens-former-nrl-ace-in-debut-contention-as-challenge-cup-21-man-squads-named-5629900 |access-date=21 March 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Jackson |first=Will |date=13 March 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers' injury doubt as Ryan Carr gives updates on Semi Valemei and Brock Greacen |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-injury-doubt-as-ryan-carr-gives-updates-on-semi-valemei-and-brock-greacen-113470/ |access-date=21 March 2026 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> Valemei made his debut in round 5, scoring a try in a heavy defeat against [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=20 March 2026 |title=Castleford receive injury boost ahead of Warrington clash as off-season recruit set for debut |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-injury-update-20-03-2026 |access-date=21 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Brennan |first=Stuart |date=21 March 2026 |title=Tanginoa scores four as Wire hammer sorry Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cz7g7r3gqxlo |access-date=21 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> The following week, he suffered a concussion in Castleford's victory against [[Bradford Bulls]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harby |first=Chris |date=26 March 2026 |title=Qareqare hat-trick helps Cas to derby win over Bulls |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cnv83618rrno |access-date=3 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=31 March 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers new boy lined up for derby debut with Valemei ruled out |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-new-boy-lined-up-for-derby-debut-with-valemei-ruled-out-6529374 |access-date=3 May 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> and missed further games in April with a knee issue.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=30 April 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers director shares comprehensive injury update pre-Hull KR as recruitment stance shared |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/rugby-league-news-castleford-tigers-director-shares-comprehensive-injury-update-as-recruitment-stance-shared-114780/ |access-date=3 May
